symptoms.symptom_name: Acute, rapid-onset severe swelling and pain of a single joint region (left midfoot/ankle)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute monoarthritis mandates exclusion of a septic joint before any other cause is accepted.
Rapid intensely painful monoarthritis of the foot is characteristic of crystal arthropathy.
Sudden single-region flare fits episodic inflammatory arthritis but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to bear weight on the affected limb

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete loss of weight-bearing reflects severe joint inflammation and raises concern for a septic joint.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever or rigors (temperature not measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever lowers but does NOT exclude septic arthritis — many septic joints are afebrile; unreliable in isolation.
Atraumatic onset makes fracture/sprain unlikely and favours an inflammatory or infective cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background of intermittent self-resolving small-joint swelling (hands/feet)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent, complete-resolution episodic small-joint arthritis is the defining pattern of palindromic rheumatism.
A known inflammatory-arthritis history does not protect against a superimposed septic joint.
Classic podagra involves the first MTP; its sparing argues somewhat against typical gout but does not exclude it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Localised soft-tissue swelling with mild (non-shiny) erythema over the joint

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Gout is typically intensely shiny-red; milder erythema is less typical but non-discriminating.
Erythema confined to the joint line rather than diffuse skin favours arthritis over cellulitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Exquisite tenderness with painful restriction of active and passive joint range of motion

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe pain on passive micro-movement is a hallmark of a septic joint; assessable only in person.

symptoms.symptom_name: Soft, non-tender calf with no popliteal tenderness; swelling joint-localised

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A soft non-tender calf with joint-localised swelling makes DVT unlikely; requires in-person leg examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Synovial-fluid aspiration: microscopy, Gram stain, culture and crystal analysis

investigation_details.investigation_name: Joint aspiration and synovial fluid analysis
investigation_details.expected_result: No organisms / negative culture and no crystals would support an inflammatory flare; a positive culture would confirm septic arthritis.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without aspiration, septic arthritis cannot be excluded — the definitive discriminating test is unavailable via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Synovial-fluid microscopy and culture is the only definitive means of excluding or confirming a septic joint.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inflammatory markers (WCC, CRP, ESR) and serum urate

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised inflammatory markers support but do not confirm infection; normal markers do not exclude it. Serum urate is often normal during an acute gout attack.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs (BP 140/85, HR 98, Temp 37.1 C, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Haemodynamically stable and afebrile on clinician measurement lowers the probability of systemic sepsis but does not exclude a localised septic joint; these are sealed clinician measurements not available to the AI Doctor.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Acute hot swollen non-weight-bearing single joint
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: This cluster defines an acute monoarthritis that must be treated as septic until excluded by aspiration, regardless of a benign background history.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urgent joint aspiration with synovial fluid microscopy, Gram stain, culture and crystal analysis (in person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The only definitive test to exclude septic arthritis and to identify crystals; mandates in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Blood tests: FBC/WCC, CRP, ESR, serum urate
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Support (not confirm) inflammation/infection; obtained at the urgent in-person visit.
Assesses for erosions, chondrocalcinosis or unsuspected bony pathology; not diagnostic for septic arthritis and should not delay aspiration.
DVT is unlikely with joint-localised swelling and a soft calf; routine Doppler is not indicated unless calf swelling or DVT features emerge.
